name: Life-Changing Testimonial
description: Person-led peer-to-peer testimonial: a relatable speaker tells their personal story — pain history, discovery moment, specific outcomes — directly to camera. 30-60s, 9:16. The story is the load-bearing element; the product is mentioned but never the visual hero. Distinct from before-after-product (product-led, reveal-driven) and doctor-authority (medical authority). The single most converting DTC ad format in 2024-2026 because peer trust beats brand trust and specific numbers beat adjectives. Reference-required when the testimonial names a real branded product; reference-optional for generic-pain-pain-solution variants.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter for the speaker keyframe — driven by either (a) a real-person persona reference (selfie / avatar) when one exists, or (b) an archetype-derived character (age, gender, vibe) when no real persona is supplied. One canonical keyframe is reused across all talking-head clips for character consistency.
stackSummary.i2v: google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter for trust-critical talking-head shots (premium lip-sync + micro-expression fidelity). kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro as the budget alternative when cost matters more than perfect lip-sync. Optional 1 × kling-v3.0-pro for a brief 3-5s pain-state b-roll cutaway when one is wanted.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — single conversational delivery across the whole video. Stability 0.4 (warmth + slight variation), similarity_boost 0.75. Tone is confiding, not performative. Language follows the user's brief.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs Scribe v1 word-level timestamps. Style depends on niche: minimal-bottom for skincare / nutra (calm, trustworthy), Hormozi-yellow word-level for fitness / software / education (high-energy, retention-driven).
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single warm low-volume bed under the entire VO at -18 to -22 dB. No tonal split (this format is one continuous emotional arc, not before/after). Optional uplift-cue at the discovery beat (15-25s) when a brighter musical lift fits the niche.

constants.structure: 0-3s pain hook → 3-15s pain detail → 15-25s discovery → 25-50s outcome → 50-60s soft CTA

variationAxes: niche (skincare / nutra / fitness / software / service / education)
pain-state visualization (talking-only / brief b-roll cutaway / split-screen before-after photos)
CTA strength (soft 'you should try it' / hard 'link in bio')
VO language (any — English, Russian, Spanish; the personal-story arc reads in any language)
doNotCopyLiterally: Scenario, VO text, speaker identity, exact numerical outcomes, product name. Vibe reference only.
referenceNotes: When the testimonial claims results from a real branded product, a product reference (logo / packaging / photo) is REQUIRED — same gate as before-after-product (AGENTS.md hard rule #3). When the speaker is a real person rather than a generic peer, a persona reference (selfie / avatar) is also required. Reference-OPTIONAL only when the format is generic-pain-pain-solution (no specific brand, no specific person). Outcome numbers must come from the user's brief, not from the model — fabricated numbers in a testimonial are an FTC and trust failure.
